title: aphyllon franciscanum
intro: Aphyllon franciscanum, also known as yellow jessamine, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural laxative and having antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Aphyllon franciscanum include its ability to treat various ailments such as constipation, indigestion, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a treatment for fever and rheumatism. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its flowers can also be dried and used in herbal remedies.
